prompt:
Fix the mirror-push mechanism in nix/valley-host.nix before the-valley migrates onto its own valley host. Today the per-project mirror hook (mirrorPusher) runs 'git push --mirror "$url"'. Against GitHub mirrors this is permanently noisy: --mirror also tries to delete the remote's read-only refs/pull/* (GitHub rejects those deletions), so EVERY mirror push exits nonzero and the hook logs 'push to $url FAILED' even though heads and tags replicated fine — replication health becomes illegible exactly when we start relying on it.

CHANGE: replace the --mirror invocation with explicit refspecs plus prune:
  git push --prune "$url" '+refs/heads/*:refs/heads/*' '+refs/tags/*:refs/tags/*'
Semantics preserved where they matter: force-updates all branches and tags, propagates deletions of branches/tags (--prune), never touches refs/pull or other remote-only namespaces. Update the module's comment where it currently says '--mirror propagates deletions, the correct semantic for a mirror' to explain the refined choice (same deletion semantics for heads/tags; --mirror rejected because read-only refs/pull on GitHub fails every push and masks real failures). Keep the setsid best-effort structure and logging exactly as is.

Check flake.nix's module-eval check: if it pins the push command text or '--mirror' anywhere, update the pin to the new invocation; if it only greps 'valley-mirrors', extend it to also pin the new refspec string so this doesn't silently regress.

Verify: nix flake check passes. PR body: the refs/pull failure mode in two sentences, the semantics comparison in one, and a note that this is deliberately the last the-valley change made through GitHub PR flow — the repo migrates to its own valley host (S1 direct-push mode) immediately after.
